meta.note: disabled by Multiview Cancellation Contract since 2026-06-19；仅作历史模板保留，默认不加载、不生成、不验收。

input_slots.source_design_document: projects/aigc/<项目名>/3-主体/场景/2-设计/S###-<场景名>.md
input_slots.source_deconstruction: <直接引用或压缩场景设计文档 ## 4. 解构 的内容>
input_slots.reference_main_image: projects/aigc/<项目名>/3-主体/场景/3-生成/<主体ID>-<主体名称>-主图.<ext>

local_sync.local_asset_path: projects/aigc/<项目名>/3-主体/场景/3-生成/<主体ID>-<主体名称>-多视图.<ext>
local_sync.download_command: libtv download -p <LIBTV_CANVAS_UUID> -n <LIBTV_NODE_ID_OR_NAME> -o projects/aigc/<项目名>/3-主体/场景/3-生成 | not_applicable_when_already_present_or_copied

reference_context.role_label: scene main image node / multiview reference

layout.sheet_intent: spatial proof board for one coherent scene, not nine separate concept shots

identity_badge.visual_style: small clean production badge, high contrast, secondary to the scene panels
identity_badge.fallback: if exact text rendering fails, preserve a clean empty badge plate for post-production overlay and keep the full name in the JSON record

must_preserve: canonical scene identity and landmark relationships
spatial skeleton, entrance direction, thresholds and circulation logic
primary light source direction, color script and atmospheric pressure
era, culture, architectural logic and material family
scale logic and environmental measurement cues
may_vary: cropping within the same spatial orientation

reference_policy.reference_main_image_role: continuity anchor for scene identity, material logic, spatial silhouette, lighting direction and color script

layout_generation_prompt.canvas_setup: Create a 16:9 widescreen scene multi-view design sheet on a clean dark neutral ground using a strict 3x3 nine-grid. Keep spacing, margins, and panel ratio uniform. Treat the sheet as a production design reference board for one coherent scene.
critical_requirements: The upstream scene design document's section `4. 解构` is the primary source of truth. Use it directly for scene identity, era, style, spatial facts, architectural logic, material logic, lighting and narrative function; do not use the former English integrated prompt from `提示词设计` as the imported source for Midjourney V8.1.

Use the same-canvas `reference_node_name` as a continuity anchor for the same scene subject; keep landmark identity, silhouette, material family, palette, lighting direction and spatial orientation consistent.
Do not redesign the scene. Do not add new architecture, new story elements, new people, creatures, silhouettes or human-shaped shadows unless the upstream section `4. 解构` explicitly requires them.
Render one coherent scene as a unified 3x3 panel sheet with strict cross-panel consistency.
Treat every panel as a view variant of the same space, not nine different scenes.
P1-P3: atmosphere plus iconography sequence: wide establishing view, medium spatial function view, close ritual or architectural detail.
P4/P5/P7/P8: structural and directional views that prove threshold design, circulation hierarchy, cosmology order, edge traversal, material joins, or sacred-light organization.
P6: low-angle up-shot emphasizing vertical authority, oppression or transcendence. P9: high-angle top-down shot revealing spatial order or cosmology logic.
Maintain one consistent spatial orientation across all panels; doors, thresholds, major landmarks and light direction must not flip randomly.
Include at least one scale-readable environmental cue such as steps, railing height, doorway size, furniture scale, floor tile rhythm or structural measurement logic, without adding people.
Panel labels, grid divisions and borders must remain clean and secondary; do not let labels, decorative UI or frame ornaments dominate the scene.

Reserve one fixed top-left identity badge area and visibly attempt the short ASCII subject ID `<SCENE_ID_ASCII_SAFE>`; if exact text rendering is unreliable, preserve the badge area for post-production overlay.

Every panel must include a small lower-left scene-view label using the panel id and short view name, such as `P1 WIDE`, `P2 MED`, `P3 DETAIL`; labels must be readable, secondary, and inside the panel margin.

layout_modules.domain_view_grammar: Scene panels must prove one reusable space through orientation, threshold, function, structure, light, circulation and top-down order.

panel_label_policy.style: small high-contrast production label, no long prose, no decorative plaque
panel_label_policy.fallback: reserve a clean lower-left label strip in every panel for post-production overlay

panel_views.label: Atmosphere Wide
panel_views.description: Eye-level wide shot establishing full spatial envelope and dominant light logic.

panel_views.label: Atmosphere Medium
panel_views.description: Mid shot showing functional zoning, narrative center and the first clear era or culture cue.

panel_views.label: Ritual Detail
panel_views.description: Close architectural or material detail proving iconography, ceremonial logic or world-specific ornament.

panel_views.label: Threshold View A
panel_views.description: Directional view proving entrance, edge traversal, barrier or passage logic.

panel_views.label: Structural View
panel_views.description: View proving architectural support, material joins, scale and spatial hierarchy.

panel_views.label: Low-Angle Vertical Axis
panel_views.description: Low-angle up-shot emphasizing vertical authority, oppression or transcendence.

panel_views.label: Circulation View
panel_views.description: View proving movement path, ritual route, service path or cosmology linkage.

panel_views.label: Material / Cultural View
panel_views.description: View foregrounding material continuity, cultural threshold, ornament or era-coded detail.

panel_views.label: High-Angle Spatial Order
panel_views.description: High-angle top-down shot revealing spatial order, zoning or cosmology logic.

consistency.label: Lighting Continuity
consistency.description: Primary light source direction and intensity remain physically coherent in all panels.

consistency.label: Material Continuity
consistency.description: Material families and texture age state remain stable across panels.

consistency.label: Layout Discipline
consistency.description: Equal panel sizes, clean gutters, no overlap and no decorative frame noise.

negative_drift_controls.identity_drift: every panel must read as the same scene with the same landmark order, material family and era logic
negative_drift_controls.orientation_drift: do not mirror doors, thresholds, dominant landmarks or primary light direction between panels
negative_drift_controls.population_drift: keep the sheet free of people, creatures, silhouettes and human-shaped shadows unless the source deconstruction explicitly requires them
negative_drift_controls.layout_drift: preserve clean 3x3 spacing and do not turn the sheet into a collage, storyboard, poster or decorative UI spread

review_focus: same scene identity across all nine panels
consistent spatial orientation, threshold placement and light direction
scale cue present without adding people
no new scene facts beyond the upstream section `4. 解构`
top-left identity badge has a visible short ID or a clean overlay-ready badge plate
each panel has a lower-left scene-view label or a clean overlay-ready label strip

negative_prompt.global: no people, no creatures, no silhouettes, no human-shaped shadows, no unrelated architecture, no scene redesign, no nine different locations, no random flipped orientation, no inconsistent era, no inconsistent materials, no unreadable clutter, no decorative UI dominance
negative_prompt.layout: no uneven grid, no overlapping panels, no broken gutters, no excessive labels, no ornamental frames dominating the scene, no missing panel label zones
negative_prompt.reference_drift: do not copy artifacts from reference image, do not introduce accidental objects, do not change the canonical scene identity

prompt_assembly.prompt_text: Use `<source_deconstruction>` as the primary scene source. Using same-canvas `<reference_node_name>` as a continuity reference, create a 16:9 3x3 spatial proof multi-view production design sheet for `<subject_name>`. Apply `subject_invariant_lock`, include the top-left identity badge with `<SCENE_ID_ASCII_SAFE>`, add lower-left scene-view labels to every panel, follow all critical requirements and panel view modules, preserve one coherent scene identity across all panels, and append `midjourney_suffix`.
